[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Mark+Lenard%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Mark+Lenard%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Mark+Lenard%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Mark+Lenard%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2013)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(born **Leonard Rosenson**,[[1]](#cite_note-1) October 15, 1924 – November 22, 1996) was an American actor, primarily in television. His most famous role was that of [Sarek](/wiki/Sarek "Sarek"), father of [Spock](/wiki/Spock "Spock"), in the science fiction *[Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek "Star Trek")* franchise, both in *[Star Trek: The Original Series](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Original_Series "Star Trek: The Original Series")* and the [animated series](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Animated_Series "Star Trek: The Animated Series"), in three films, and in two episodes of *[Star Trek: The Next Generation](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Next_Generation "Star Trek: The Next Generation")*. He also played a [Klingon](/wiki/Klingon "Klingon") in *[Star Trek The Motion Picture](/wiki/Star_Trek_The_Motion_Picture "Star Trek The Motion Picture")*, and a [Romulan](/wiki/Romulan "Romulan") Commander in the episode "[Balance of Terror](/wiki/Balance_of_Terror "Balance of Terror")" of the original series.[[2]](#cite_note-Mark_Lenard_|work=IMDb-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor (1924–1996)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Mark Lenard"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Mark+Lenard%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Mark+Lenard%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Mark+Lenard%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Mark+Lenard%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Mark+Lenard%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Mark+Lenard%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2013)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Mark Lenard | |

| Lenard (left) as [Sarek](/wiki/Sarek "Sarek") | |

**Mark Lenard** (born **Leonard Rosenson**,[[1]](#cite_note-1) October 15, 1924 – November 22, 1996) was an American actor, primarily in television. His most famous role was that of [Sarek](/wiki/Sarek "Sarek"), father of [Spock](/wiki/Spock "Spock"), in the science fiction *[Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek "Star Trek")* franchise, both in *[Star Trek: The Original Series](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Original_Series "Star Trek: The Original Series")* and the [animated series](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Animated_Series "Star Trek: The Animated Series"), in three films, and in two episodes of *[Star Trek: The Next Generation](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Next_Generation "Star Trek: The Next Generation")*. He also played a [Klingon](/wiki/Klingon "Klingon") in *[Star Trek The Motion Picture](/wiki/Star_Trek_The_Motion_Picture "Star Trek The Motion Picture")*, and a [Romulan](/wiki/Romulan "Romulan") Commander in the episode "[Balance of Terror](/wiki/Balance_of_Terror "Balance of Terror")" of the original series.[[2]](#cite_note-Mark_Lenard_|work=IMDb-2)

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mark_Lenard&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Lenard was born in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"),[[3]](#cite_note-Spelling-3) the son of a [Russian Jewish](/wiki/Russian_Jewish "Russian Jewish") immigrant, Abraham, and his wife, Bessie, and was raised in the small town of [South Haven](/wiki/South_Haven,_Michigan "South Haven, Michigan"), Michigan, on [Lake Michigan](/wiki/Lake_Michigan "Lake Michigan"), where his family owned a tourist resort.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) He joined the [United States Army](/wiki/United_States_Army "United States Army") in 1943 and trained to be a paratrooper during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"); he did not see combat, and was discharged in 1946 as a technical sergeant.[[6]](#cite_note-fardreaming-6)

He got his start on stage while in the army. After earning a master's degree in theater and speech from the [University of Michigan](/wiki/University_of_Michigan "University of Michigan"), he became known in New York City for serious drama, including the plays of Ibsen, Shaw, and Chekhov. His first noteworthy role was that of Conrad in [John Gielgud](/wiki/John_Gielgud "John Gielgud")'s production of *[Much Ado About Nothing](/wiki/Much_Ado_About_Nothing "Much Ado About Nothing")*, followed by the Broadway show *[A Far Country](/wiki/A_Far_Country_(play) "A Far Country (play)")*.[[3]](#cite_note-Spelling-3)[[7]](#cite_note-7) In the mid-1960s, he moved his family to Los Angeles, where he played the recurring role of Dr. Ernest Gregory in *[Another World](/wiki/Another_World_(TV_series) "Another World (TV series)")*, and one of the [Three Wise Men](/wiki/Biblical_Magi "Biblical Magi") in the biblical epic *[The Greatest Story Ever Told](/wiki/The_Greatest_Story_Ever_Told "The Greatest Story Ever Told")* (1965).[[6]](#cite_note-fardreaming-6)

Lenard is best known for his appearances in the *Star Trek* franchise, particularly in the role of Sarek, the father of Spock ([Leonard Nimoy](/wiki/Leonard_Nimoy "Leonard Nimoy")). His first *Star Trek* appearance was in the first season of *[Star Trek: The Original Series](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Original_Series "Star Trek: The Original Series")*, however, playing the first [Romulan](/wiki/Romulan "Romulan") ever seen in the series, in the episode "[Balance of Terror](/wiki/Balance_of_Terror "Balance of Terror")" (1966). He originated the character of Sarek in the second-season episode "[Journey to Babel](/wiki/Journey_to_Babel "Journey to Babel")" (1967), and provided the voice of Sarek in the *[Star Trek: The Animated Series](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Animated_Series "Star Trek: The Animated Series")* episode "[Yesteryear](/wiki/Yesteryear_(Star_Trek:_The_Animated_Series) "Yesteryear (Star Trek: The Animated Series)")" (1973). He later played a [Klingon](/wiki/Klingon "Klingon") Captain in *[Star Trek: The Motion Picture](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Motion_Picture "Star Trek: The Motion Picture")* (1979), which gave him the distinction of being the first actor to play a Romulan, a Vulcan, and a Klingon in *Star Trek*. He reprised the role of Sarek in three of the *Star Trek* feature films—*[Star Trek III: The Search for Spock](/wiki/Star_Trek_III:_The_Search_for_Spock "Star Trek III: The Search for Spock")* (1984), *[Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home](/wiki/Star_Trek_IV:_The_Voyage_Home "Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home")* (1986), and *[Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country](/wiki/Star_Trek_VI:_The_Undiscovered_Country "Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country")* (1991)—and provided the voice of young Sarek in a brief flashback sequence in *[Star Trek V: The Final Frontier](/wiki/Star_Trek_V:_The_Final_Frontier "Star Trek V: The Final Frontier")* (1989). Additionally, he appeared as the elderly Sarek in the third-season *[Star Trek: The Next Generation](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Next_Generation "Star Trek: The Next Generation")* episode "[Sarek](/wiki/Sarek_(Star_Trek:_The_Next_Generation) "Sarek (Star Trek: The Next Generation)")" (1990) and the fifth-season episode "[Unification: Part 1](/wiki/Unification_(Star_Trek:_The_Next_Generation) "Unification (Star Trek: The Next Generation)")" (1991).[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Lenard began acting in television series dramas and TV movies in 1955. He was the fifth actor to play Nathan Walsh in the soap opera *[Search for Tomorrow](/wiki/Search_for_Tomorrow "Search for Tomorrow")* (1959–1960). He played Dr. Ernest Gregory in *[Another World](/wiki/Another_World_(TV_series) "Another World (TV series)")* (1964–1965). For two seasons Lenard played Aaron Stempel in the television series *[Here Come the Brides](/wiki/Here_Come_the_Brides "Here Come the Brides")* (1968–1970).[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Lenard guest-starred in several episodes of the original *[Mission: Impossible](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(1966_TV_series) "Mission: Impossible (1966 TV series)")* from 1966 to 1970, including one with Leonard Nimoy, and in a two-part episode of *[Buck Rogers in the 25th Century](/wiki/Buck_Rogers_in_the_25th_Century_(TV_series) "Buck Rogers in the 25th Century (TV series)").* He played the prosecutor in Fort Grant in the [Clint Eastwood](/wiki/Clint_Eastwood "Clint Eastwood") film *[Hang 'Em High](/wiki/Hang_%27Em_High "Hang 'Em High")* (1968), and the hostile gorilla Urko in the television series *[Planet of the Apes](/wiki/Planet_of_the_Apes_(TV_series) "Planet of the Apes (TV series)")*. He made a guest appearance on *[Little House on the Prairie](/wiki/Little_House_on_the_Prairie_(TV_series) "Little House on the Prairie (TV series)")*, in the episode "Journey in the Spring, Part I", playing Peter Ingalls, older brother of [Charles Ingalls](/wiki/Charles_Ingalls "Charles Ingalls"). He had roles in *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")* several times, including the episode "Nowhere to Run" (1968).[[2]](#cite_note-Mark_Lenard_|work=IMDb-2)

Lenard played a lead role in the film *Noon Sunday*, filmed on [Guam](/wiki/Guam "Guam") with his costars [Keye Luke](/wiki/Keye_Luke "Keye Luke"); [John Russell](/wiki/John_Russell_(actor) "John Russell (actor)") of the series *[Lawman](/wiki/Lawman_(TV_series) "Lawman (TV series)")*; and character actor [Stacy Harris](/wiki/Stacy_Harris "Stacy Harris"). In *The Radicals* (1990), which recounted the beginnings of the Swiss [Anabaptist](/wiki/Anabaptist "Anabaptist") movement in the 1520s, he played a composite historical character, Eberhard Hoffman, a Catholic bishop who serves as prosecutor in the trial of his former abbot [Michael Sattler](/wiki/Michael_Sattler "Michael Sattler").[[10]](#cite_note-10) In 1993, Lenard and fellow *Star Trek* actor [Walter Koenig](/wiki/Walter_Koenig "Walter Koenig") toured in a production of *The Boys in Autumn*. Lenard played a late middle-aged [Huck Finn](/wiki/Huck_Finn "Huck Finn") who re-encounters his childhood friend [Tom Sawyer](/wiki/Tom_Sawyer "Tom Sawyer") after a lifetime apart. Koenig played Sawyer.

Lenard died aged 72, of [multiple myeloma](/wiki/Multiple_myeloma "Multiple myeloma") in New York City.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mark_Lenard&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Filmography")]

### Film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mark_Lenard&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Film")]

* *[The Greatest Story Ever Told](/wiki/The_Greatest_Story_Ever_Told "The Greatest Story Ever Told")* (1965) - Balthazar
* *[Hang 'Em High](/wiki/Hang_%27Em_High "Hang 'Em High")* (1968) - Prosecuting Attorney at Fort Grant
* *[Noon Sunday](/wiki/Noon_Sunday "Noon Sunday")* (1970) - Jason Cootes
* *[Annie Hall](/wiki/Annie_Hall "Annie Hall")* (1977) - Navy Officer
* *[Getting Married](/wiki/Getting_Married_(1978_film) "Getting Married (1978 film)")* (1978) - Mr. Bloom
* *[Star Trek: The Motion Picture](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Motion_Picture "Star Trek: The Motion Picture")* (1979) - Klingon Captain
* *[Star Trek III: The Search for Spock](/wiki/Star_Trek_III:_The_Search_for_Spock "Star Trek III: The Search for Spock")* (1984) - Ambassador [Sarek](/wiki/Sarek "Sarek")
* *[Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home](/wiki/Star_Trek_IV:_The_Voyage_Home "Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home")* (1986) - Ambassador Sarek
* *[Star Trek V: The Final Frontier](/wiki/Star_Trek_V:_The_Final_Frontier "Star Trek V: The Final Frontier")* (1989) - Ambassador Sarek (voice)
* *The Radicals* (1990) - Eberhard Hoffmann
* *[Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country](/wiki/Star_Trek_VI:_The_Undiscovered_Country "Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country")* (1991) - Ambassador Sarek

### Television

* *Studio One* (1960) - David
* *[Search for Tomorrow](/wiki/Search_for_Tomorrow "Search for Tomorrow")* (1959–1960) - Nathan Walsh
* *[Armstrong Circle Theatre](/wiki/Armstrong_Circle_Theatre "Armstrong Circle Theatre")*: "Ghost Bomber: The Lady Be Good" (1960) - Major Bennett
* *Family Classics: The Three Musketeers* (1960) - Jusac De Rochefort
* *[Another World](/wiki/Another_World_(TV_series) "Another World (TV series)")* (1964–1965) - Dr. Ernest Gregory
* *[Mission: Impossible](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(1966_TV_series) "Mission: Impossible (1966 TV series)")*: "[Wheels](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(season_1) "Mission: Impossible (season 1)")" (S1:E7, 1966) - Felipe Mora
* *[Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Original_Series "Star Trek: The Original Series")*: "[Balance of Terror](/wiki/Balance_of_Terror "Balance of Terror")" (S1:E14, 1966) - [Romulan](/wiki/Romulan "Romulan") Commander
* *[Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Original_Series "Star Trek: The Original Series")*: "[Journey to Babel](/wiki/Journey_to_Babel "Journey to Babel")" (S2:E10, 1967) - [Sarek](/wiki/Sarek "Sarek")
* *[Mission: Impossible](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(1966_TV_series) "Mission: Impossible (1966 TV series)")*: "[Trek](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(season_2) "Mission: Impossible (season 2)")" (S2:E2, 1967) - Col. Luis Cardoza
* *[The Wild Wild West](/wiki/The_Wild_Wild_West "The Wild Wild West")*: "The Night of the Iron Fist" (S3:E14, 1967) - Count Draja

* *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*: "No Where to Run" (S13:E18, 1968) - Ira Stonecipher
* *[Here Come the Brides](/wiki/Here_Come_the_Brides "Here Come the Brides")* (1968–1970) - Aaron Stempel
* *[Mission: Impossible](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(1966_TV_series) "Mission: Impossible (1966 TV series)")*: "[Nitro](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(season_3) "Mission: Impossible (season 3)")" (S3:E21, 1969) - Aristo Skora
* *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*: "To Hell with Babe Ruth" (1969) - Yoshio Nagata
* *[Mission: Impossible](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(1966_TV_series) "Mission: Impossible (1966 TV series)")*: "[The Rebel](/wiki/Mission:_Impossible_(season_5) "Mission: Impossible (season 5)")" (1970) - Colonel Bakram
* *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*: "Will the Real Mr. Winkler Please Die" (S5:E19, 1973) - Rogloff
